question: The label of a herbarium sheet does not carry information on (NEET-II 2016)
explanation: A herbarium is a collection of plants, which have been dried, pressed, mounted on herbarium sheets, identified and classified. A printed label (7 × 12 cm) giving the following information is fixed on the lower, right corner of herbarium sheet: (i) Scientific name of plant (ii) Common/vernacular name (iii) Family (iv) Locality (v) Date of collection (vi) Collection number (vii) Name of collector (viii) Plant characteristics (optional) (ix) Name of institution (optional). It does not carry the height of the plant.

question: Match column I with column II for housefly classification and select the correct option using the codes given below: A. Family, B. Order, C. Class, D. Phylum; (i) Diptera, (ii) Arthropoda, (iii) Muscidae, (iv) Insecta. (NEET-II 2016)
explanation: The correct taxonomic classification for a housefly is: Family - Muscidae, Order - Diptera, Class - Insecta, and Phylum - Arthropoda.

question: Study the four statements (A-D) given below and select the two correct ones out of them: A. Definition of biological species was given by Ernst Mayr. B. Photoperiod does not affect reproduction in plants. C. Binomial nomenclature system was given by R.H. Whittaker. D. In unicellular organisms, reproduction is synonymous with growth. (NEET-II 2016)
explanation: Photoperiod affects flowering and reproduction in plants. Binomial nomenclature system was given by Carolus Linnaeus. Thus, statements A and D are correct.

question: Nomenclature is governed by certain universal rules. Which one of the following is contrary to the rules of nomenclature? (NEET-I 2016)
options: The names are written in Latin and are italicised.
When written by hand the names are to be underlined.
Biological names can be written in any language.
The first word in a biological name represents the genus name and the second is a specific epithet.
explanation: Biological names are derived either from Latin language or are latinised. This is because Latin language is a dead language and therefore it will not change in form or spellings with the passage of time.

question: Which one of the following is not a correct statement? (NEET 2013)
options: A museum has collection of photographs of plants and animals.
Key is a taxonomic aid for identification of specimens.
Herbarium houses dried, pressed and preserved plant specimens.
Botanical gardens have collection of living plants for reference.
explanation: Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions. Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens. They do not primarily collect photographs.

question: The common characteristics between tomato and potato will be maximum at the level of their (Karnataka NEET 2013)
explanation: Potato (Solanum tuberosum) and tomato (Lycopersicum esculentum) both belong to family Solanaceae, which is commonly called as the “potato family”.

question: Which one of the following organisms is scientifically correctly named, correctly printed according to the International Rules of Nomenclature and correctly described? (Mains 2012)
options: Musca domestica - the common house lizard, a reptile
Plasmodium falciparum - a protozoan pathogen causing the most serious type of malaria.
Felis tigris - the Indian tiger, well protected in Gir forests.
E.coli - full name Entamoeba coli, a commonly occurring bacterium in human intestine.
explanation: Plasmodium falciparum is a protozoan parasite that causes the most dangerous form of malaria (malignant tertian or pernicious malaria) in humans. Musca domestica is the housefly, not a lizard; Panthera tigris is the Indian tiger; and E. coli stands for Escherichia coli.

question: Which one of the following animals is correctly matched with its particular taxonomic category? (2011)
explanation: The zoological name of the tiger is Panthera tigris. In the binomial nomenclature system, the first part is the generic name and the second is the specific name. So, tigris is the species name.

question: Which one of the following aspects is an exclusive characteristic of living things? (Mains 2011)
options: Isolated metabolic reactions occur in vitro
Increase in mass from inside only
Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory.
Increase in mass by accumulation of material both on surface as well as internally.
explanation: Living organisms are uniquely distinguished by their ability to perceive environmental stimuli and process them, often retaining a memory of these events.

question: ICBN stands for (2007)
explanation: The International Code of Botanical Nomenclature (ICBN) is a set of rules and recommendations dealing with the formal botanical names given to plants.

question: The living organisms can be unexceptionally distinguished from the non-living things on the basis of their ability for (2007)
options: interaction with the environment and progressive evolution
explanation: Consciousness or responsiveness to external stimuli (like touch) is considered a defining property of living organisms that distinguishes them from non-living things.

question: One of the most important functions of botanical gardens is that (2005)
options: they provide a beautiful area for recreation
one can observe tropical plants there
they allow ex situ conservation of germplasm
they provide the natural habitat for wild life.
explanation: Ex situ conservation means “offsite conservation”. Botanical gardens serve this role by maintaining collections of germplasm to protect rare and endemic plant species.

question: Species are considered as (2003)
options: artificial concept of human mind which cannot be defined in absolute terms
real units of classification devised by taxonomists.
explanation: Species is a natural population of individuals which are genetically distinct and reproductively isolated. It is considered the basic unit of taxonomy.

question: Biosystematics aims at (2003)
options: the classification of organisms based on broad morphological characters
delimiting various taxa of organisms and establishing their relationships
the classification of organisms based on their evolutionary history and establishing their phylogeny on the totality of various parameters from all fields of studies
identification and arrangement of organisms on the basis of their cytological characteristics.
explanation: Biosystematics is the study of the diversity of organisms and their comparative and evolutionary relationships based on parameters from fields like comparative anatomy, ecology, physiology, and biochemistry.

question: Which of the following is less general in characters as compared to genus? (2001)
explanation: In the taxonomic hierarchy (kingdom, division, class, order, family, genus, species), species is the lowest category. The number of common characters is maximum at this level, and characters are less general compared to higher categories.

question: The book ‘Genera Plantarum’ was written by (1999)
explanation: Bentham and Hooker authored the monumental three-volume work Genera Plantarum (1862-1883), which provided elaborate keys for the identification of 202 natural orders and genera.

question: ‘Taxon’ is the unit of a group of (1996)
explanation: Taxon refers to all categories in the taxonomic hierarchy. It represents any level of grouping of organisms, such as kingdom, class, order, family, genus, or species.

question: Linnaeus is credited with (1993)
explanation: The binomial nomenclature system of scientific naming was first introduced by Carolus Linnaeus in his works Systema Naturae (1735) and Species Plantarum (1753).

question: Which is the correct sequence of taxonomic categories? (1992)
explanation: The standard hierarchy of classification proceeds as: Species → Genus → Tribe → Family → Order → Class → Division/Phylum → Kingdom.

question: The term phylum was given by (1992)
explanation: The term 'phylum' was coined by Georges Cuvier.

question: A group of plants or animals with similar traits of any rank is (1992, 1991)
explanation: A taxon is a name designating an organism or group of organisms assigned to a particular rank in a systematic hierarchy reflecting evolutionary relationships.

question: A taxon is (1992, 1990)
options: a taxonomic group of any ranking.
explanation: The word taxon signifies a taxonomic group of any rank that represents the real biological organisms included in a category.

question: Basic unit or smallest taxon of taxonomy/ classification is (1990)
explanation: The basic unit or smallest taxon in biological classification is the species. It is a group of individuals that remain relatively constant in their characteristics and do not normally interbreed with other species.

question: Linnaeus evolved a system of nomenclature called (1990)
explanation: Linnaeus devised the binomial nomenclature system, which uses two Latin words for an organism's scientific name (generic name and specific name).

question: The term “New Systematics” was introduced by (1988)
explanation: The term “New Systematics” was introduced by Julian Huxley in 1940. This approach takes into account various characters such as cytological, morphological, genetical, and physiological traits.

question: Static concept of species was put forward by (1988)
explanation: The static concept of species, which suggested that species were fixed and unchanging, was a very early botanical idea associated with Theophrastus.
